MUNICIPAL INFRASTRUCTURE STANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURES (SOP)
Document ID: SOP-SAFE-2025-V3
Subject: Pedestrian Safety and Signalized Junction Compliance
 
1. PEDESTRIAN CROSSING TIMING POLICIES
All signalized intersections operating within municipal limits must maintain a minimum pedestrian walk clearance interval. The standard calculation for the pedestrian clearance interval (Flashing Don't Walk) is based on a walking speed of 3.5 feet per second (fps) across the total width of the crosswalk.

2. HIGH-ACCIDENT LOCATION (HAL) PROTOCOLS
Any intersection that records more than 5 vehicular collisions or 2 pedestrian-related incidents within a 12-month calendar window is automatically designated as a High-Accident Location (HAL). 
 
Mandatory Actions for HAL Intersections:
1. Immediate physical site audit by a certified Traffic Safety Engineer within 14 business days.
2. Implementation of

cal site audit by a certified Traffic Safety Engineer within 14 business days.
2. Implementation of Leading Pedestrian Intervals (LPI), giving pedestrians a 3-to-7 second head start before vehicular traffic gets a green light.
3. Review of night-time visibility and street lighting lumens.
